title: The Core Diffusion Idea
concept: Take a clean image, slowly corrupt it into pure noise, then train a network to reverse that process step by step, denoising noise back into an image.

exercises.prompt: Have you ever watched TV static or fog slowly clear up into a picture? What do you think is happening in reverse?

options.text: Someone is painting the picture from scratch with a brush
The blur/noise is being removed little by little to reveal a clear image
The picture was always clear, it was just hidden behind a curtain

exercises.explain: That's exactly the idea behind diffusion models! They start with pure noise and remove a little bit of it at a time until a clear image appears.

cards.title: From Chaos... to a Picture?
cards.body: Imagine dropping a photo into a blender until it's just fuzzy noise. Now imagine an AI that can watch that happen backwards — and rebuild the photo from scratch, out of nothing but static. That's not magic, it's diffusion!

cards.title: What Is Diffusion?
cards.body: Diffusion is a way to train an AI by: 1) taking a clean image and slowly adding noise until it becomes pure random static, then 2) teaching a network to reverse this — removing noise step by step until a clean image appears again. Once trained, the AI can start from random noise alone and 'denoise' its way into a brand new image.

cards.title: Melting Ice, Rewound
cards.body: Think of an ice sculpture melting into a puddle. Now imagine watching a video of that melting in reverse — the puddle slowly reforms into the sculpture. Diffusion models learn what 'melting' (adding noise) looks like, so they can run the video backwards (removing noise) to sculpt an image out of a random puddle of static.

cards.title: Turning Static into a Cat
cards.body: Start with a photo of a cat. Add tiny bits of random noise again and again until it's just gray static — no cat visible at all. A diffusion model studies this whole process thousands of times. Then, given fresh random static, it removes noise step by step, guessing what a 'less noisy' version would look like, until a brand new cat image appears.

cards.title: Myth: The AI Draws the Image Directly
cards.body: Myth: Diffusion models paint an image in one shot, like a brush stroke. Truth: They never draw anything directly. Instead, they take many small denoising steps — each one just slightly less noisy than the last — until a clear image slowly emerges from pure randomness.

cards.title: Two Directions, One Idea
cards.body: Diffusion has a forward process (adding noise) used for training, and a reverse process (removing noise) used for creating images.

left.text: Start with a clean image and gradually sprinkle in random noise until it becomes pure static. This is how the AI learns.

right.text: Start with pure static and gradually remove noise, step by step, until a brand new clean image appears.

exercises.prompt: What does a diffusion model learn to do during training?

options.text: Memorize every pixel of every training image
Reverse the process of turning a clean image into noise
Compress images into smaller file sizes
Sort images into categories like cats and dogs

exercises.explain: Diffusion models are trained to reverse noise-adding — that is, to predict how to remove a little noise at each step, eventually turning static back into a clear image.

exercises.prompt: A diffusion model creates a brand new image by starting with random noise and removing it step by step.
exercises.explain: True! Once trained, diffusion models generate new images by starting from pure noise and gradually denoising it into a clear picture.

exercises.sentence: In the forward process, a clean image is slowly turned into pure ___ by adding a little bit at each step.
exercises.explain: The forward process gradually adds noise to a clean image until nothing recognizable is left — just random static.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes the REVERSE process in diffusion models?

options.text: Removing noise step by step to reveal a clear image
Adding noise step by step until the image disappears
Copying a clean image pixel by pixel

exercises.explain: The reverse process is the denoising direction: starting from noise and gradually cleaning it up into a picture — this is how new images get generated.

exercises.prompt: A diffusion model is given a canvas of pure random static and asked to generate an image of a mountain. What will it do first?

options.text: Draw the outline of a mountain immediately in one step
Slightly reduce the noise, taking a tiny step toward a clearer image
Search the internet for a real mountain photo to copy
Add even more noise to the static first

exercises.explain: Diffusion models never jump straight to the final image. They take many small denoising steps, each one slightly clearer than the last, gradually revealing the mountain out of the static.
